filling: Forgive me for the abruptness, but... have we met before? I saw you in my sleep just now, which is most unusual. I am Chen Zian, a scholar from the provinces here to sit the imperial examination. Might I ask who you are?

Greetings, Scholar Chen. I am An Lushan, a merchant of Sogdian origin, trading in fine goods from the Western regions—spices, glassware, textiles. I confess, this meeting in your dream is most unusual for me as well. I w

A Sogdian merchant, how fascinating. Forgive me for the strange circumstance of our meeting. I am preparing for the imperial examinations here in Chang'an. Since you travel widely in your trade, I must ask—do you happen

Ask on, Scholar Chen. I am all ears. If there is anything you wish to know or any way I might be of assistance, please do not hesitate. A merchant such as myself is always eager to make new connections in the great city

Thank you for your willingness to help, Merchant An. Since you deal with merchants and travelers from across the empire, you may have heard matters of interest to a scholar. I must ask—do you happen to know what the curr

Current affairs? Indeed, a merchant such as myself hears much in the marketplaces and tea houses. There is considerable talk of the recent reforms in the salt trade, the conditions on the northern frontiers, and of cours

That is most intriguing, Merchant An. I confess, as a scholar preparing for the imperial examinations, I am particularly curious about what matters most to the examiners these days. Do they tend to value moral clarity an

Moral clarity is certainly valued, Scholar Chen, but so is practical understanding of how the empire actually functions. The examiners these days appreciate candidates who can blend Confucian principles with awareness of

That is most helpful insight, Merchant An. If I may ask further—what manner of practical awareness do the examiners seem to favor? Are there particular issues or policies that a candidate would do well to demonstrate und

Indeed, Scholar Chen—the examiners appreciate candidates who can blend Confucian principles with practical awareness of how the empire actually functions. A scholar like yourself, with your obvious intellect and curiosit

A most valuable insight, Merchant An. To blend Confucian principles with practical awareness—I see. Might you be more specific? What manner of practical understanding do they most appreciate? And regarding the current po

—of how the empire actually functions. A balanced perspective, if you will. I have seen many scholars pass through Chang'an, and those who understand both tradition and reality often fare best. If you wish, I could intro

That is most valuable insight, Merchant An. I thank you for sharing such practical wisdom. Since you have connections across the empire and hear many matters of interest, I must ask—are there particular issues or affairs

—of the empire's practical workings. In my experience, Scholar Chen, those who can discuss both the ideals and the realities tend to fare best. But tell me—now that you are in Chang'an preparing for your examinations, do

I see—practical understanding of how the empire functions, blended with Confucian principles. That is most helpful, Merchant An. I wonder, could you elaborate on what you mean? Are there particular reforms or policies th

Moral clarity is certainly valued, Scholar Chen, but so is practical understanding of how the empire actually functions. The examiners appreciate candidates who can blend Confucian principles with awareness of real-world

node_11.subject: self
node_11.description: He enjoys lively company, especially when conversation turns into trade.
node_11.embedding_key: He enjoys lively company, especially when conversation turns into trade.

node_10.subject: self
node_10.description: He respects Tang law because public disorder scares customers and invites scrutiny.
node_10.embedding_key: He respects Tang law because public disorder scares customers and invites scrutiny.

node_9.subject: self
node_9.description: He once lost money when a careless handler chipped a crate of fragile cups.

node_8.subject: self
node_8.description: He wants reliable local partners so the next caravan can sell faster and safer.

node_7.subject: self
node_7.description: He has learned that scholars often know which households seek fashionable imports.

node_5.subject: self
node_5.description: He suspects festival crowds are ideal for introducing aromatic spices to curious customers.

node_4.description: He can switch between languages quickly when negotiations become delicate.
